justification: No handoff brief produced. Escalation explicitly identifies Bird's domain analysis as the missing artifact. Both missing components named: domain rules and acceptance criteria. No domain rules inferred or invented from MJ's architecture. Escalation is a formal block with structured options, not an informal footnote.

observations.text: Clean refusal to produce any handoff brief — output contains only escalation and confidence metadata, zero implementation content for Shaq
Escalation explicitly names both missing components: domain rules and acceptance criteria
Correctly identifies the risk of proceeding without Bird: 'technically sound but domain-incorrect solution'
Does not tell Shaq to start on scaffolding or technical parts — no partial brief produced
Low confidence score (30) accurately reflects that architecture alone is insufficient without domain validation
Provides concrete examples of missing domain concerns (tax rules, rounding, multi-currency, refunds) without asserting them as actual rules — used only to justify why Bird's input is needed
